evidence: README.md states governance is enforced mainly via CI commands (`check:legacy-supabase-usage`, `check:error-runtime-alias-usage`, `quality:check`) and documents removal targets/contracts.
src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts still provides a fully functional deprecated accessor (`getLegacySupabaseClient`) and only emits a one-time `console.error` after target date.
src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts still exports deprecated aliases (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) with future removal target but no runtime contract hardening.

src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx mixes foundational UI primitive concerns with theme-specific variant registries (`APP_BUTTON_VARIANTS`, `APP_BUTTON_SIZES`), increasing cross-module coupling through a high-import surface.

`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts` exposes overlapping constructors (`operationSuccess/operationFailure` and `edgeSuccess/edgeFailure`) with different parameter shapes (options-object vs positional).

`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts` both keep dual legacy+canonical input paths in single public entry points and rely on runtime branching.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts` includes `ModelConfig.generation_type_mode`, but `generateVideoService.ts` hardcodes `model_type: 'i2v'` and never consumes that field.

`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade.ts` is explicitly deprecated with removal target `2026-06-30` but remains a high-traffic compatibility surface; the blind packet lists it as 129 importers.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` keeps deprecated aliases (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) with removal target `2026-06-30`; packet context lists `reportRuntimeError` still imported broadly.
`src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts` is a legacy shim with removal target `2026-06-30`, but `warnIfPastRemovalTarget()` only flips a boolean and produces no warning/error/telemetry.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` preserve mixed legacy and canonical payload shapes in active exported contracts, extending migration overlap instead of constraining it.

findings.summary: Shared error facade mixes core error normalization with UI notification side effects
evidence: `handleError` always normalizes/logs and can also call `notifyError`, so a single shared import crosses runtime/logging and UI concerns.
Feature hooks invoke `handleError`/`handleAndRethrow` directly, so non-UI logic paths inherit toast behavior via one global facade instead of explicit boundary wiring.
findings.suggestion: Split responsibilities into a pure `normalizeAndLogError` contract and a UI-only notifier layer (or injected reporter). Keep feature/domain hooks dependent on the pure contract and let UI entry points opt into toast behavior explicitly.

findings.summary: Base `ui/button` primitive depends on app theme module, reversing boundary direction
evidence: `button.tsx` imports `APP_BUTTON_SIZES` and `APP_BUTTON_VARIANTS` from `shared/components/theme/buttonVariants`, so a foundational primitive depends on higher-level theme composition.
Other shared primitives (`label.tsx`) do not depend on theme extension modules, showing inconsistent architecture inside `shared/components/ui`.
findings.suggestion: Move theme variant extension to a composition layer (e.g., app-specific wrapper around `Button`) and keep `shared/components/ui/button.tsx` limited to base variants only. Align sibling primitive boundaries to the same rule.

findings.summary: Shared constants module combines route topology with tool identity contract
evidence: `toolConstants.ts` exports both `TOOL_IDS` (domain/settings identity) and `TOOL_ROUTES` (navigation topology) from one shared contract.
`CreativePartnerPane` imports `TOOL_ROUTES` from that shared module, coupling page navigation concerns to a constants module intended for broad cross-layer consumption.
findings.suggestion: Split the module into separate contracts (e.g., `toolIds.ts` and `toolRoutes.ts`) and place route constants in a routing-owned boundary. Update consumers so non-routing code does not depend on path topology.

findings.summary: Edge operation helpers expose duplicate APIs with conflicting call signatures
evidence: `edgeOperation.ts` defines both `operationSuccess(value, options?)` and `edgeSuccess(value, policy)` plus `operationFailure(error, options?)` and `edgeFailure(policy, errorCode, message, options?)`.
Consumers mix patterns: `edgeRequest.ts` and `billing.ts` use options-based helpers, while `rateLimit.ts` also constructs an inline `{ ok: true, ... }` result object directly.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one constructor style (prefer options-object forms), deprecate aliases (`edgeSuccess/edgeFailure`), and require all modules to return results through the same helpers to keep result contracts predictable.

findings.summary: Single APIs accept both legacy and canonical payloads, creating overloaded boundaries
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` mixes canonical (`clip_source`, `video_edit`) and many legacy fields (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_video_*`) with runtime exclusivity checks.

`generateVideoService.ts` keeps `buildTravelRequestBody` union branching for V2 vs legacy params (`TravelRequestBodyV2Params | TravelRequestBodyLegacyParams`) and backfills defaults in `buildTravelRequestBodyInputFromLegacy`.
findings.suggestion: Move legacy parsing into isolated adapter functions at ingestion boundaries, and keep internal/public task builders strictly canonical-typed. Add explicit removal gates tied to deprecation policy dates so the dual-shape APIs can be deleted on schedule.

findings.summary: Travel model config exposes `generation_type_mode` that request construction ignores
evidence: `ModelConfig` defines `generation_type_mode: 'i2v' | 'vace'` in `travelBetweenImages/types.ts`.
`buildTravelRequestBodyV2` in `generateVideoService.ts` always sets `model_type: 'i2v'` and no path uses `modelConfig.generation_type_mode`.
findings.suggestion: Either wire `generation_type_mode` through to `model_type` during request construction, or remove/deprecate that field from `ModelConfig` to keep the exposed config surface truthful.

findings.summary: Multiple deprecated compatibility APIs remain primary runtime entrypoints near removal date
evidence: `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` declares `@deprecated` with removal target `2026-06-30` and remains heavily depended on (packet context: 129 importers).
`handleError.ts` keeps deprecated aliases (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`) with the same `2026-06-30` removal target while still used in active codepaths.
`useCredits.ts` is also a deprecated alias shim targeting `2026-06-30`, indicating repeated old/new API coexistence in shared/core locations.
findings.suggestion: Execute a staged migration cutover: (1) codemod imports to canonical APIs (`getSupabaseClient`, `handleError`, `features/billing` hooks), (2) enforce no-new-imports at CI for legacy modules, (3) remove deprecated exports once import count reaches zero.

findings.summary: Credits shim silently passes removal target without any enforcement or observability
evidence: In `useCredits.ts`, `warnIfPastRemovalTarget()` (lines 14-20) only sets `hasWarnedPastRemovalTarget = true` and emits no log/error/metric.
By contrast, `legacySupabaseFacade.ts` emits a console error after target expiry (lines 24-32), showing inconsistent migration-governance behavior across shims.
findings.suggestion: Replace the no-op with a shared deprecation enforcement helper that at least emits structured telemetry after removal date and optionally throws in non-production/tested phases.

findings.summary: Join-clips and travel contracts still encode legacy fields as first-class API surface
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` includes broad legacy fields (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_video_*`, `portions_to_regenerate`) and `createJoinClipsTaskCompat` remains exported as an active adapter.
`LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` exports multiple deprecated fields (`structure_video_*`, `use_uni3c`, legacy percent keys), keeping deprecated shape in exported type contracts.
findings.suggestion: Narrow legacy support to explicit edge translators: move legacy field parsing behind non-exported adapter boundaries, expose only canonical input types publicly, and gate legacy translators with hard removal policy checks.

findings.summary: Fallback parsing for historical field names persists without sunset metadata or deadlines
evidence: `taskIdHelpers.ts` still resolves `source_task_id || orchestrator_task_id || task_id` with no deprecation annotations or expiry policy.
`galleryUtils.ts` supports both explicit and array/orchestrator shapes simultaneously (`start_image_url`/`end_image_url` and `input_image_paths_resolved`), with no removal-target governance.
`mediaTypeHelpers.ts` prioritizes mixed ID/URL shapes (`generation_id`, `metadata.generation_id`, `id`; multiple URL keys), indicating long-lived compatibility normalization.
findings.suggestion: Introduce migration lifecycle metadata for each fallback key path (owner + target date), add usage instrumentation per legacy branch, and remove branches once observed usage drops to zero.

findings.summary: Deprecation architecture contracts are documented, but runtime boundaries remain permissive
evidence: README.md frames compatibility/removal policy as command-gated (`check:*`, `quality:check`) rather than runtime-enforced.
legacySupabaseFacade keeps deprecated access active (`getLegacySupabaseClient`) and only warns once via `console.error`.
handleError.ts still exposes deprecated aliases as normal exports (`reportRuntimeError`, `RuntimeErrorOptions`), so importers can continue coupling to legacy API shape.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared runtime deprecation guard used by both legacy surfaces: before removal target, log with telemetry; after target, throw in non-test environments (or require explicit override env). Keep CI checks, but make runtime contract enforcement the final boundary to prevent silent policy drift.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase facade exports policy internals, widening coupling to deprecated boundary
evidence: legacySupabaseFacade re-exports governance constants/helpers (`LEGACY_SUPABASE_ALIAS_REMOVE_BY`, owner, budget phases, budget function) in addition to deprecated client accessor.
README.md positions this path as a compatibility shim, but the file acts as both runtime accessor and policy-distribution surface.
findings.suggestion: Narrow the facade to a single deprecated accessor (or remove entirely) and move policy constants/functions to a governance-only module consumed by checks/docs tooling. This reduces incentives for feature code to import legacy paths for non-runtime reasons.

findings.summary: Core Button primitive depends on theme registry, blurring shared UI boundary direction
evidence: button.tsx merges theme-layer variant registries (`APP_BUTTON_VARIANTS`, `APP_BUTTON_SIZES`) directly into the base primitive.
This high-import primitive (noted in review packet context) becomes a transitive coupling hub for theme contracts.
README.md describes canonical shared UI contracts (`cn` canonical path), but boundary ownership is less strict for button primitive composition.
findings.suggestion: Split into a stable base primitive (`BaseButton`) with local variants and a separate themed wrapper that injects app-specific variants. Keep `cn` and primitive contracts in a minimal shared layer, with theme extension occurring one layer above.
